macro_context.market_sentiment: Strongly bullish. S&P 500 hit a fresh all-time high (+0.5%) and Nasdaq gained 1.1% on May 1 driven by Apple's Q2 earnings beat. April was the best month since 2020 for both indices with ~10% gains, and 10 of 11 sectors finished positive.
macro_context.fed_outlook: Fed held rates steady at 3.5-3.75% on April 29 in a contentious 8-4 vote (last 4-dissent split since Oct 1992). Easing bias was included in the statement. Powell steps down as chair May 15 but stays on the Board; leadership transition to Kevin Warsh adds near-term policy uncertainty. No rate cut imminent given elevated inflation.
macro_context.geopolitical: Middle East war threatens Strait of Hormuz, driving energy price spikes and elevated global inflation (IMF: 4.4% in 2026). U.S. tariff uncertainty, Venezuela intervention, and NATO posture in Greenland add complexity. Dimon himself warned risks could 'eclipse anything since WWII.' Oil fell slightly May 1 on peace-deal progress signals via Pakistani mediators.
key_events: Fed chair transition: Powell exits May 15, Kevin Warsh takes over
Middle East ceasefire negotiations ongoing — outcome affects energy/inflation
IMF projects global growth only 3.1% in 2026 under base case
Apple earnings beat sparked broad tech-led rally on May 1

ticker_news.headline: Dimon warns of 'some kind of bond crisis' ahead as global debt risks build
JPMorgan Q1 2026 EPS $5.94 beats $5.49 estimate by 8.2%; FICC trading revenue +21% to $7.1B
JPM raises S&P 500 year-end target to 7,600 and lifts 2026 firmwide NII guidance to ~$104.5B
JPMorganChase annual shareholder meeting set for May 19, 2026; Dimon to present at Bernstein conference May 27
Dimon reveals JPMorgan is considering offering prediction market services

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: 27 analysts tracked: 11 Strong Buy, 2 Moderate Buy, 14 Hold, 0 Sell. Most recent price target: Evercore ISI at $340 on April 17, 2026 (implies ~8.4% upside). Median target $345, range $257.89-$420. Last downgrade was Oppenheimer in November 2024 — no 2026 downgrades found.

web_context.competitor_activity: All six major U.S. banks beat Q1 capital markets expectations. Goldman Sachs posted record equities trading revenue. BofA is expanding cross-sell between consumer and wealth management segments. Citigroup stock surpassed net asset value for first time in 7 years, signaling a turnaround. JPM and Wells Fargo are both trading at record highs, maintaining leadership. JPM's fastest-rising DCM competitor identified as Morgan Stanley, not Citi (per eFinancialCareers). Broad sector tailwind from strong investment banking and trading environment.
web_context.earnings_info: Next earnings: Q2 2026, expected July 14, 2026. Q2 2026 EPS consensus estimate: $5.36-$5.41. Most recent reported: Q1 2026 EPS $5.94 (beat $5.49 estimate by 8.2%, reported April 14, 2026). No Q2 revenue consensus found in search results.
